title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: wpgdata provides simple R interface query download datasets City Winnipeg Open Data Portal using OData V4 API. vignette walks core functions using Assessment Parcels dataset (d4mq-wa44) working example.
code: library(wpgdata) library(dplyr)

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: typical wpgdata workflow follows five steps:
code: peg_catalogue()   →   peg_info()   →   peg_metadata()   →   peg_query()   →   peg_all() (find datasets)       (explore)        (find fields)        (filter)          (download all)

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: working dataset, use peg_catalogue() browse available datasets portal. best starting point don’t yet know dataset ID: Count datasets category get overview ’s available: Search name find specific dataset retrieve ID: Use id value peg_* function:
code: catalogue <- peg_catalogue() catalogue #> # A tibble: 200 × 7 #>    name           id    description category updated_at          row_count url   #>    <chr>          <chr> <chr>       <chr>    <dttm>                  <int> <chr> #>  1 WFPS Call Logs yg42… "The data … Fire an… 2026-03-08 16:31:12      1349 http… #>  2 Plow Zone Sch… tix9… "Scheduled… City Pl… 2026-03-08 16:25:50       630 http… #>  3 311 Call Wait… vrzk… "Caller wa… Contact… 2026-03-08 16:25:29       701 http… #>  4 Council Votin… f9mn… "On Septem… Council… 2026-03-08 16:14:49       347 http… #>  5 River Water L… tgrf… "Record of… Water a… 2026-03-08 16:01:44       731 http… #>  6 Accessibility… fxq5… "This data… Streets  2026-03-08 16:00:58        67 http… #>  7 FIPPA Request… pfbi… "The Freed… Organiz… 2026-03-08 11:41:58       484 http… #>  8 WPA Paystation b85e… "Onstreet … Parking  2026-03-08 10:03:40       385 http… #>  9 Walkways       jdeq… "This data… City Pl… 2026-03-08 10:03:29       326 http… #> 10 Daily Adult M… du7c… "The data … Insect … 2026-03-08 10:00:15      1445 http… #> # ℹ 190 more rows catalogue |>   dplyr::count(category, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 26 × 2 #>    category                                                   n #>    <chr>                                                  <int> #>  1 Census                                                    29 #>  2 City Planning                                             25 #>  3 Development Approvals, Building Permits, & Inspections    23 #>  4 Transportation Planning & Traffic Management              18 #>  5 Uncategorized                                             14 #>  6 Council Services                                          13 #>  7 Organizational Support Services                            8 #>  8 Assessment, Taxation, & Corporate                          7 #>  9 Contact Centre - 311                                       7 #> 10 Water and Waste                                            7 #> # ℹ 16 more rows catalogue |>   dplyr::filter(grepl("assessment", name, ignore.case = TRUE)) |>   dplyr::select(name, id, updated_at) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   name               id        updated_at          #>   <chr>              <chr>     <dttm>              #> 1 Assessment Parcels d4mq-wa44 2026-03-08 09:32:08 dataset_id <- catalogue |>   dplyr::filter(name == "Assessment Parcels") |>   dplyr::pull(id)  dataset_id #> [1] "d4mq-wa44"

what: Step 2 — Explore a dataset with peg_info()
title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Use peg_info() understand dataset contains — name, description, category, update frequency, row count: tells us dataset last updated, many times downloaded, license published — useful committing large download.
code: peg_info("d4mq-wa44") #> # A tibble: 1 × 11 #>   name        description category created_at rows_updated_at view_last_modified #>   <chr>       <chr>       <chr>    <date>     <date>          <date>             #> 1 Assessment… List of al… Assessm… 2017-08-23 2026-03-08      2026-03-08         #> # ℹ 5 more variables: view_count <int>, download_count <int>, tags <list>, #> #   license <chr>, provenance <chr>

what: Step 3 — Find field names with peg_metadata()
title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: OData queries require exact field names. Use peg_metadata() look available fields types writing query: field_name column contains names use peg_query(). type column tells whether field text, number, another type — important writing correct filter expressions. example, find numeric fields :
code: meta <- peg_metadata("d4mq-wa44") meta #> # A tibble: 71 × 4 #>    name               field_name         type   description #>    <chr>              <chr>              <chr>  <chr>       #>  1 Roll Number        roll_number        text   NA          #>  2 Street Number      street_number      number NA          #>  3 Unit Number        unit_number        text   NA          #>  4 Street Suffix      street_suffix      text   NA          #>  5 Street Direction   street_direction   text   NA          #>  6 Street Name        street_name        text   NA          #>  7 Street Type        street_type        text   NA          #>  8 Full Address       full_address       text   NA          #>  9 Neighbourhood Area neighbourhood_area text   NA          #> 10 Market Region      market_region      text   NA          #> # ℹ 61 more rows meta |>   dplyr::filter(type == "number") #> # A tibble: 24 × 4 #>    name                            field_name                  type  description #>    <chr>                           <chr>                       <chr> <chr>       #>  1 Street Number                   street_number               numb… NA          #>  2 Total Living Area               total_living_area           numb… In Square … #>  3 Year Built                      year_built                  numb… NA          #>  4 Rooms                           rooms                       numb… NA          #>  5 Number Floors (Condo)           number_floors_condo         numb… NA          #>  6 Assessed Land Area              assessed_land_area          numb… In Square … #>  7 Water Frontage Measurement      water_frontage_measurement  numb… in Feet     #>  8 Sewer Frontage Measurement      sewer_frontage_measurement  numb… In Feet     #>  9 Total Assessed Value            total_assessed_value        numb… NA          #> 10 Total Proposed Assessment Value total_proposed_assessment_… numb… NA          #> # ℹ 14 more rows

what: Step 4 — Fetch a quick preview with peg_get()
title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: peg_get() fetches first page dataset (server’s default page size). useful quick look data structure writing targeted queries: Check column names types:
code: df <- suppressWarnings(peg_get("d4mq-wa44")) df #> # A tibble: 1,000 × 72 #>    `__id`   roll_number street_number unit_number street_suffix street_direction #>    <chr>    <chr>               <int> <chr>       <lgl>         <lgl>            #>  1 row-iai… 01000001000          1636 NA          NA            NA               #>  2 row-u7e… 01000005500          1584 NA          NA            NA               #>  3 row-rxq… 01000008000          1574 NA          NA            NA               #>  4 row-7nj… 01000008200          1550 NA          NA            NA               #>  5 row-pte… 01000008400          1538 NA          NA            NA               #>  6 row-ehq… 01000008500          1536 NA          NA            NA               #>  7 row-iyr… 01000013200          1520 NA          NA            NA               #>  8 row-78v… 01000013300          1510 NA          NA            NA               #>  9 row-cv3… 01000013600          1500 NA          NA            NA               #> 10 row-trz… 01000013700          1490 NA          NA            NA               #> # ℹ 990 more rows #> # ℹ 66 more variables: street_name <chr>, street_type <chr>, #> #   full_address <chr>, neighbourhood_area <chr>, market_region <chr>, #> #   total_living_area <int>, building_type <chr>, basement <chr>, #> #   basement_finish <chr>, year_built <int>, rooms <int>, #> #   air_conditioning <chr>, fire_place <chr>, attached_garage <chr>, #> #   detached_garage <chr>, pool <chr>, number_floors_condo <int>, … dplyr::glimpse(df) #> Rows: 1,000 #> Columns: 72 #> $ `__id`                          <chr> "row-iaim.ytxs-dh5m", "row-u7eu_kbti.y… #> $ roll_number                     <chr> "01000001000", "01000005500", "0100000… #> $ street_number                   <int> 1636, 1584, 1574, 1550, 1538, 1536, 15… #> $ unit_number                     <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA… #> $ street_suffix                   <lgl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA… #> $ street_direction                <lgl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA… #> $ street_name                     <chr> "MCCREARY", "MCCREARY", "MCCREARY", "M… #> $ street_type                     <chr> "ROAD", "ROAD", "ROAD", "ROAD", "ROAD"… #> $ full_address                    <chr> "1636 MCCREARY ROAD", "1584 MCCREARY R… #> $ neighbourhood_area              <chr> "WILKES SOUTH", "WILKES SOUTH", "WILKE… #> $ market_region                   <chr> "6, CHARLESWOOD", "6, CHARLESWOOD", "6… #> $ total_living_area               <int> 1313, 4007, 1052, 3120, 1510, 4570, 49… #> $ building_type                   <chr> "ONE STOREY", "TWO STOREY", "ONE STORE… #> $ basement                        <chr> "Yes", "Yes", "No", "Yes", "Yes", "Yes… #> $ basement_finish                 <chr> "No", "Yes", "No", "No", "Yes", "Yes",… #> $ year_built                      <int> 1991, 1991, 2007, 1982, 1970, 1958, 20… #> $ rooms                           <int> 5, 8, 5, 6, 5, 8, 10, 9, 6, 8, 5, 10, … #> $ air_conditioning                <chr> "Yes", "Yes", "Yes", "Yes", "Yes", "Ye… #> $ fire_place                      <chr> "No", "Yes", "No", "No", "Yes", "Yes",… #> $ attached_garage                 <chr> "No", "Yes", "No", "Yes", "No", "No", … #> $ detached_garage                 <chr> "Yes", "No", "Yes", "No", "No", "Yes",… #> $ pool                            <chr> "No", "No", "No", "No", "No", "No", "Y… #> $ number_floors_condo             <int> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA… #> $ property_use_code               <chr> "RESSD - DETACHED SINGLE DWELLING", "R… #> $ assessed_land_area              <int> 197030, 218155, 43628, 130705, 130718,… #> $ water_frontage_measurement      <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA… #> $ sewer_frontage_measurement      <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA… #> $ property_influences             <chr> "EXTERNAL CORNER,NO SEWER NO WATER", "… #> $ zoning                          <chr> "A - AGRICULTURAL", "A - AGRICULTURAL"… #> $ total_assessed_value            <int> 723000, 1619000, 570000, 743000, 57700… #> $ total_proposed_assessment_value <int> 893000, 1994000, 650000, 968000, 66300… #> $ assessment_date                 <chr> "2023-04-01T00:00:00.000", "2023-04-01… #> $ detail_url                      <chr> "Some(http://www.winnipegassessment.co… #> $ current_assessment_year         <int> 2026, 2026, 2026, 2026, 2026, 2026, 20… #> $ property_class_1                <chr> "RESIDENTIAL 1", "RESIDENTIAL 1", "RES… #> $ status_1                        <chr> "TAXABLE", "TAXABLE", "TAXABLE", "TAXA… #> $ assessed_value_1                <int> 723000, 1619000, 570000, 743000, 57700… #> $ property_class_2                <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA… #> $ status_2 NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA… #> $ status_3                        <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA… #> $ assessed_value_3                <int> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA… #> $ property_class_4                <lgl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA… #> $ status_4                        <lgl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA… #> $ assessed_value_4 title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: peg_query() supports five OData parameters:

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Pass R expressions directly — wpgdata translates OData: use raw OData strings prefer: approaches return identical results.
code: peg_query("d4mq-wa44",   filter = total_assessed_value > 1000000,   top    = 10 ) #> # A tibble: 10 × 72 #>    `__id`   roll_number street_number unit_number street_suffix street_direction #>    <chr>    <chr>               <int> <lgl>       <lgl>         <lgl>            #>  1 row-u7e… 01000005500          1584 NA          NA            NA               #>  2 row-iyr… 01000013200          1520 NA          NA            NA               #>  3 row-ygb… 01000014500          1450 NA          NA            NA               #>  4 row-bp9… 01000045500          1290 NA          NA            NA               #>  5 row-nsk… 01000064000          1820 NA          NA            NA               #>  6 row-y94… 01000067500          1916 NA          NA            NA               #>  7 row-s2m… 01000067900          1892 NA          NA            NA               #>  8 row-fzv… 01000092200          1700 NA          NA            NA               #>  9 row-4c9… 01000096000          1720 NA          NA            NA               #> 10 row-44b… 01000306500          2424 NA          NA            NA               #> # ℹ 66 more variables: street_name <chr>, street_type <chr>, #> #   full_address <chr>, neighbourhood_area <chr>, market_region <chr>, #> #   total_living_area <int>, building_type <chr>, basement <chr>, #> #   basement_finish <chr>, year_built <int>, rooms <int>, #> #   air_conditioning <chr>, fire_place <chr>, attached_garage <chr>, #> #   detached_garage <chr>, pool <chr>, number_floors_condo <lgl>, #> #   property_use_code <chr>, assessed_land_area <int>, … peg_query("d4mq-wa44",   filter = "total_assessed_value gt 1000000",   top    = 10 ) #> # A tibble: 10 × 72 #>    `__id`   roll_number street_number unit_number street_suffix street_direction #>    <chr>    <chr>               <int> <lgl>       <lgl>         <lgl>            #>  1 row-u7e… 01000005500          1584 NA          NA            NA               #>  2 row-iyr… 01000013200          1520 NA          NA            NA               #>  3 row-ygb… 01000014500          1450 NA          NA            NA               #>  4 row-bp9… 01000045500          1290 NA          NA            NA               #>  5 row-nsk… 01000064000          1820 NA          NA            NA               #>  6 row-y94… 01000067500          1916 NA          NA            NA               #>  7 row-s2m… 01000067900          1892 NA          NA            NA               #>  8 row-fzv… 01000092200          1700 NA          NA            NA               #>  9 row-4c9… 01000096000          1720 NA          NA            NA               #> 10 row-44b… 01000306500          2424 NA          NA            NA               #> # ℹ 66 more variables: street_name <chr>, street_type <chr>, #> #   full_address <chr>, neighbourhood_area <chr>, market_region <chr>, #> #   total_living_area <int>, building_type <chr>, basement <chr>, #> #   basement_finish <chr>, year_built <int>, rooms <int>, #> #   air_conditioning <chr>, fire_place <chr>, attached_garage <chr>, #> #   detached_garage <chr>, pool <chr>, number_floors_condo <lgl>, #> #   property_use_code <chr>, assessed_land_area <int>, …

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Combine conditions & () | ():
code: peg_query("d4mq-wa44",   filter = total_assessed_value > 1000000 & building_type == "TWO STOREY",   top    = 10 ) #> # A tibble: 10 × 72 #>    `__id`   roll_number street_number unit_number street_suffix street_direction #>    <chr>    <chr>               <int> <lgl>       <lgl>         <lgl>            #>  1 row-u7e… 01000005500          1584 NA          NA            NA               #>  2 row-iyr… 01000013200          1520 NA          NA            NA               #>  3 row-ygb… 01000014500          1450 NA          NA            NA               #>  4 row-nsk… 01000064000          1820 NA          NA            NA               #>  5 row-3vg… 01000560000          3179 NA          NA            NA               #>  6 row-mrq… 01000615000             3 NA          NA            NA               #>  7 row-zir… 01000615400            17 NA          NA            NA               #>  8 row-uym… 01000615800            31 NA          NA            NA               #>  9 row-txp… 01000617400            36 NA          NA            NA               #> 10 row-eh9… 01000718800           400 NA          NA            NA               #> # ℹ 66 more variables: street_name <chr>, street_type <chr>, #> #   full_address <chr>, neighbourhood_area <chr>, market_region <chr>, #> #   total_living_area <int>, building_type <chr>, basement <chr>, #> #   basement_finish <chr>, year_built <int>, rooms <int>, #> #   air_conditioning <chr>, fire_place <chr>, attached_garage <chr>, #> #   detached_garage <chr>, pool <chr>, number_floors_condo <lgl>, #> #   property_use_code <chr>, assessed_land_area <int>, …

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Use select return columns need:
code: peg_query("d4mq-wa44",   select = c("roll_number", "full_address", "total_assessed_value",              "building_type", "year_built"),   top    = 10 ) #> # A tibble: 10 × 5 #>    roll_number full_address       building_type  year_built total_assessed_value #>    <chr>       <chr>              <chr>               <int>                <int> #>  1 01000001000 1636 MCCREARY ROAD ONE STOREY           1991               723000 #>  2 01000005500 1584 MCCREARY ROAD TWO STOREY           1991              1619000 #>  3 01000008000 1574 MCCREARY ROAD ONE STOREY           2007               570000 #>  4 01000008200 1550 MCCREARY ROAD ONE STOREY           1982               743000 #>  5 01000008400 1538 MCCREARY ROAD ONE STOREY           1970               577000 #>  6 01000008500 1536 MCCREARY ROAD 4 LEVEL SPLIT        1958               979000 #>  7 01000013200 1520 MCCREARY ROAD TWO STOREY           2021              1900000 #>  8 01000013300 1510 MCCREARY ROAD ONE & 1/2 STO…       2000               995000 #>  9 01000013600 1500 MCCREARY ROAD ONE & 1/2 STO…       1994               669000 #> 10 01000013700 1490 MCCREARY ROAD CABOVER              2008               882000

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Use orderby sort ascending descending:
code: peg_query("d4mq-wa44",   select  = c("roll_number", "full_address", "total_assessed_value"),   orderby = "total_assessed_value desc",   top     = 10 ) #> # A tibble: 10 × 3 #>    roll_number full_address                total_assessed_value #>    <chr>       <chr>                                      <int> #>  1 13099071230 1485 PORTAGE AVENUE                    651316000 #>  2 03091643600 92 DYSART ROAD                         475244000 #>  3 08020955700 1225 ST MARY'S ROAD                    328848000 #>  4 13096152000 700 WILLIAM AVENUE                     262782000 #>  5 12092819100 10 KENNEDY STREET                      262044000 #>  6 12093468100 242 HARGRAVE STREET                    214972000 #>  7 06072082500 409 TACHE AVENUE                       206873000 #>  8 10006776045 555 STERLING LYON PARKWAY              200244000 #>  9 07055050000 T-35-2000 WELLINGTON AVENUE            180099000 #> 10 09010473150 1555 REGENT AVENUE W                   162335000

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
code: peg_query("d4mq-wa44",   filter  = total_assessed_value > 1000000 & year_built > 2000,   select  = c("roll_number", "full_address", "total_assessed_value",               "building_type", "year_built"),   orderby = "total_assessed_value desc",   top     = 10 ) #> # A tibble: 10 × 5 #>    roll_number full_address        building_type year_built total_assessed_value #>    <chr>       <chr>               <chr>              <int>                <int> #>  1 10004062400 23 KERSLAKE PLACE   TWO STOREY          2006              4235000 #>  2 10003003000 137 HANDSART BOULE… TWO STOREY          2006              4077000 #>  3 10002999100 214 GRENFELL BOULE… TWO STOREY          2009              4054000 #>  4 10006777080 36 AVONLYNN COURT   ONE STOREY          2022              3905000 #>  5 12040844000 885 WELLINGTON CRE… TWO STOREY          2021              3855000 #>  6 10002760000 135 PARK BOULEVARD… TWO STOREY          2023              3643000 #>  7 01002770500 70 RIDGEDALE CRESC… TWO STOREY          2024              3486000 #>  8 10002884000 130 HANDSART BOULE… TWO STOREY          2020              3386000 #>  9 10006776560 124 GRENFELL BOULE… TWO STOREY          2017              3335000 #> 10 10003006000 123 HANDSART BOULE… TWO STOREY          2005              3304000

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Use skip top together paginate manually:
code: # rows 1-5 page_1 <- peg_query("d4mq-wa44", top = 5, skip = 0)  # rows 6-10 page_2 <- peg_query("d4mq-wa44", top = 5, skip = 5)

what: Step 6 — Download all rows with peg_all()
title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: peg_all() automatically paginates pages returns single tibble. shows progress bar row counts percentage: large datasets, default max_pages = 10 prevents accidental downloads. Set max_pages = Inf fetch everything:
code: # fetch up to 3 pages (safety cap) peg_all("d4mq-wa44", max_pages = 3) #> # A tibble: 41,000 × 72 #>    `__id`   roll_number street_number unit_number street_suffix street_direction #>    <chr>    <chr>               <int> <chr>       <chr>         <chr>            #>  1 row-iai… 01000001000          1636 NA          NA            NA               #>  2 row-u7e… 01000005500          1584 NA          NA            NA               #>  3 row-rxq… 01000008000          1574 NA          NA            NA               #>  4 row-7nj… 01000008200          1550 NA          NA            NA               #>  5 row-pte… 01000008400          1538 NA          NA            NA               #>  6 row-ehq… 01000008500          1536 NA          NA            NA               #>  7 row-iyr… 01000013200          1520 NA          NA            NA               #>  8 row-78v… 01000013300          1510 NA          NA            NA               #>  9 row-cv3… 01000013600          1500 NA          NA            NA               #> 10 row-trz… 01000013700          1490 NA          NA            NA               #> # ℹ 40,990 more rows #> # ℹ 66 more variables: street_name <chr>, street_type <chr>, #> #   full_address <chr>, neighbourhood_area <chr>, market_region <chr>, #> #   total_living_area <int>, building_type <chr>, basement <chr>, #> #   basement_finish <chr>, year_built <int>, rooms <int>, #> #   air_conditioning <chr>, fire_place <chr>, attached_garage <chr>, #> #   detached_garage <chr>, pool <chr>, number_floors_condo <int>, … # fetch all rows - may take several minutes for large datasets peg_all("d4mq-wa44", max_pages = Inf)

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: easiest way use peg_catalogue() directly R: Alternatively, find IDs City Winnipeg Open Data Portal: Browse data.winnipeg.ca Open dataset Click API → OData V4 Copy last segment URL:
code: peg_catalogue() |>   dplyr::filter(grepl("your search term", name, ignore.case = TRUE)) |>   dplyr::select(name, id, category) https://data.winnipeg.ca/api/odata/v4/d4mq-wa44                                       ^^^^^^^^^^                                       dataset ID

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: City Winnipeg Open Data Portal Socrata Developer Portal OData V4 Query Options

title: Authors and Citation
text: Myo Minn Oo. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Oo M (2026). wpgdata: Access Winnipeg Open Data Portal via OData V4. R package version 0.2.0, https://myominnoo.github.io/wpgdata.
code: @Manual{,   title = {wpgdata: Access the Winnipeg Open Data Portal via OData V4},   author = {Myo Minn Oo},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://myominnoo.github.io/wpgdata}, }

title: wpgdata
text: wpgdata provides simple R interface query download datasets City Winnipeg Open Data Portal using OData V4 API.

title: wpgdata
text: Install CRAN: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("wpgdata") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("myominnoo/wpgdata")

title: wpgdata
text: typical workflow follows steps:
code: library(wpgdata) peg_catalogue()   →   peg_info()   →   peg_metadata()   →   peg_query()   →   peg_all() (find datasets)       (explore)        (find fields)        (filter)          (download all)

title: wpgdata
text: List datasets Winnipeg Open Data Portal IDs, categories, last updated dates: Filter category search name find dataset ID:
code: peg_catalogue() #> # A tibble: 200 × 7 #>    name           id    description category updated_at          row_count url   #>    <chr>          <chr> <chr>       <chr>    <dttm>                  <int> <chr> #>  1 WFPS Call Logs yg42… "The data … Fire an… 2026-03-08 16:31:12      1349 http… #>  2 Plow Zone Sch… tix9… "Scheduled… City Pl… 2026-03-08 16:25:50       630 http… #>  3 311 Call Wait… vrzk… "Caller wa… Contact… 2026-03-08 16:25:29       701 http… #>  4 Council Votin… f9mn… "On Septem… Council… 2026-03-08 16:14:49       347 http… #>  5 River Water L… tgrf… "Record of… Water a… 2026-03-08 16:01:44       731 http… #>  6 Accessibility… fxq5… "This data… Streets  2026-03-08 16:00:58        67 http… #>  7 FIPPA Request… pfbi… "The Freed… Organiz… 2026-03-08 11:41:58       484 http… #>  8 WPA Paystation b85e… "Onstreet … Parking  2026-03-08 10:03:40       385 http… #>  9 Walkways       jdeq… "This data… City Pl… 2026-03-08 10:03:29       326 http… #> 10 Daily Adult M… du7c… "The data … Insect … 2026-03-08 10:00:15      1445 http… #> # ℹ 190 more rows library(dplyr)  # count datasets by category peg_catalogue() |>   count(category, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 26 × 2 #>    category                                                   n #>    <chr>                                                  <int> #>  1 Census                                                    29 #>  2 City Planning                                             25 #>  3 Development Approvals, Building Permits, & Inspections    23 #>  4 Transportation Planning & Traffic Management              18 #>  5 Uncategorized                                             14 #>  6 Council Services                                          13 #>  7 Organizational Support Services                            8 #>  8 Assessment, Taxation, & Corporate                          7 #>  9 Contact Centre - 311                                       7 #> 10 Water and Waste                                            7 #> # ℹ 16 more rows  # search by name peg_catalogue() |>   filter(grepl("assessment", name, ignore.case = TRUE)) |>   select(name, id, updated_at) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   name               id        updated_at          #>   <chr>              <chr>     <dttm>              #> 1 Assessment Parcels d4mq-wa44 2026-03-08 09:32:08

title: wpgdata
text: Get high-level information dataset downloading :
code: peg_info("d4mq-wa44") #> # A tibble: 1 × 11 #>   name        description category created_at rows_updated_at view_last_modified #>   <chr>       <chr>       <chr>    <date>     <date>          <date>             #> 1 Assessment… List of al… Assessm… 2017-08-23 2026-03-08      2026-03-08         #> # ℹ 5 more variables: view_count <int>, download_count <int>, tags <list>, #> #   license <chr>, provenance <chr>

title: wpgdata
text: Look field names types querying. Always use field_name values peg_query():
code: peg_metadata("d4mq-wa44") #> # A tibble: 71 × 4 #>    name               field_name         type   description #>    <chr>              <chr>              <chr>  <chr>       #>  1 Roll Number        roll_number        text   <NA>        #>  2 Street Number      street_number      number <NA>        #>  3 Unit Number        unit_number        text   <NA>        #>  4 Street Suffix      street_suffix      text   <NA>        #>  5 Street Direction   street_direction   text   <NA>        #>  6 Street Name        street_name        text   <NA>        #>  7 Street Type        street_type        text   <NA>        #>  8 Full Address       full_address       text   <NA>        #>  9 Neighbourhood Area neighbourhood_area text   <NA>        #> 10 Market Region      market_region      text   <NA>        #> # ℹ 61 more rows

title: wpgdata
text: Fetch first page dataset. Warns rows available:
code: suppressWarnings(peg_get("d4mq-wa44")) #> # A tibble: 1,000 × 72 #>    `__id`   roll_number street_number unit_number street_suffix street_direction #>    <chr>    <chr>               <int> <chr>       <lgl>         <lgl>            #>  1 row-iai… 01000001000          1636 <NA>        NA            NA               #>  2 row-u7e… 01000005500          1584 <NA>        NA            NA               #>  3 row-rxq… 01000008000          1574 <NA>        NA            NA               #>  4 row-7nj… 01000008200          1550 <NA>        NA            NA               #>  5 row-pte… 01000008400          1538 <NA>        NA            NA               #>  6 row-ehq… 01000008500          1536 <NA>        NA            NA               #>  7 row-iyr… 01000013200          1520 <NA>        NA            NA               #>  8 row-78v… 01000013300          1510 <NA>        NA            NA               #>  9 row-cv3… 01000013600          1500 <NA>        NA            NA               #> 10 row-trz… 01000013700          1490 <NA>        NA            NA               #> # ℹ 990 more rows #> # ℹ 66 more variables: street_name <chr>, street_type <chr>, #> #   full_address <chr>, neighbourhood_area <chr>, market_region <chr>, #> #   total_living_area <int>, building_type <chr>, basement <chr>, #> #   basement_finish <chr>, year_built <int>, rooms <int>, #> #   air_conditioning <chr>, fire_place <chr>, attached_garage <chr>, #> #   detached_garage <chr>, pool <chr>, number_floors_condo <int>, …

title: wpgdata
text: Query dataset using R expressions raw OData strings:
code: # R expression filter peg_query("d4mq-wa44",   filter = total_assessed_value > 1000000,   top    = 5 ) #> Warning: ! Not all rows returned. The dataset is paginated. #> ℹ Rows retrieved: 5 #> ℹ Use `peg_all()` to retrieve all rows. #> # A tibble: 5 × 72 #>   `__id`    roll_number street_number unit_number street_suffix street_direction #>   <chr>     <chr>               <int> <lgl>       <lgl>         <lgl>            #> 1 row-u7eu… 01000005500          1584 NA          NA            NA               #> 2 row-iyrb… 01000013200          1520 NA          NA            NA               #> 3 row-ygb8… 01000014500          1450 NA          NA            NA               #> 4 row-bp9n… 01000045500          1290 NA          NA            NA               #> 5 row-nskr… 01000064000          1820 NA          NA            NA               #> # ℹ 66 more variables: street_name <chr>, street_type <chr>, #> #   full_address <chr>, neighbourhood_area <chr>, market_region <chr>, #> #   total_living_area <int>, building_type <chr>, basement <chr>, #> #   basement_finish <chr>, year_built <int>, rooms <int>, #> #   air_conditioning <chr>, fire_place <chr>, attached_garage <chr>, #> #   detached_garage <chr>, pool <chr>, number_floors_condo <lgl>, #> #   property_use_code <chr>, assessed_land_area <int>, … # select specific columns peg_query("d4mq-wa44",   select = c("roll_number", "full_address", "total_assessed_value"),   top    = 5 ) #> Warning: ! Not all rows returned. The dataset is paginated. #> ℹ Rows retrieved: 5 #> ℹ Use `peg_all()` to retrieve all rows. #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   roll_number full_address       total_assessed_value #>   <chr>       <chr>                             <int> #> 1 01000001000 1636 MCCREARY ROAD               723000 #> 2 01000005500 1584 MCCREARY ROAD              1619000 #> 3 01000008000 1574 MCCREARY ROAD               570000 #> 4 01000008200 1550 MCCREARY ROAD               743000 #> 5 01000008400 1538 MCCREARY ROAD               577000 # sort results peg_query("d4mq-wa44",   select  = c("roll_number", "full_address", "total_assessed_value"),   orderby = "total_assessed_value desc",   top     = 5 ) #> Warning: ! Not all rows returned. The dataset is paginated. #> ℹ Rows retrieved: 5 #> ℹ Use `peg_all()` to retrieve all rows. #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   roll_number full_address        total_assessed_value #>   <chr>       <chr>                              <int> #> 1 13099071230 1485 PORTAGE AVENUE            651316000 #> 2 03091643600 92 DYSART ROAD                 475244000 #> 3 08020955700 1225 ST MARY'S ROAD            328848000 #> 4 13096152000 700 WILLIAM AVENUE             262782000 #> 5 12092819100 10 KENNEDY STREET              262044000 # combine filter + select + orderby peg_query("d4mq-wa44",   filter  = total_assessed_value > 1000000,   select  = c("roll_number", "full_address", "total_assessed_value"),   orderby = "total_assessed_value desc",   top     = 5 ) #> Warning: ! Not all rows returned. The dataset is paginated. #> ℹ Rows retrieved: 5 #> ℹ Use `peg_all()` to retrieve all rows. #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   roll_number full_address        total_assessed_value #>   <chr>       <chr>                              <int> #> 1 13099071230 1485 PORTAGE AVENUE            651316000 #> 2 03091643600 92 DYSART ROAD                 475244000 #> 3 08020955700 1225 ST MARY'S ROAD            328848000 #> 4 13096152000 700 WILLIAM AVENUE             262782000 #> 5 12092819100 10 KENNEDY STREET              262044000

what: peg_all() — fetch all rows with pagination
title: wpgdata
text: Retrieve rows across multiple pages progress bar:
code: peg_all("d4mq-wa44", max_pages = 3) #> Warning: ! Stopped after 3 pages. #> ℹ Rows retrieved : 41000 of 245130 #> ℹ Use `max_pages = Inf` to fetch all. #> # A tibble: 41,000 × 72 #>    `__id`   roll_number street_number unit_number street_suffix street_direction #>    <chr>    <chr>               <int> <chr>       <chr>         <chr>            #>  1 row-iai… 01000001000          1636 <NA>        <NA>          <NA>             #>  2 row-u7e… 01000005500          1584 <NA>        <NA>          <NA>             #>  3 row-rxq… 01000008000          1574 <NA>        <NA>          <NA>             #>  4 row-7nj… 01000008200          1550 <NA>        <NA>          <NA>             #>  5 row-pte… 01000008400          1538 <NA>        <NA>          <NA>             #>  6 row-ehq… 01000008500          1536 <NA>        <NA>          <NA>             #>  7 row-iyr… 01000013200          1520 <NA>        <NA>          <NA>             #>  8 row-78v… 01000013300          1510 <NA>        <NA>          <NA>             #>  9 row-cv3… 01000013600          1500 <NA>        <NA>          <NA>             #> 10 row-trz… 01000013700          1490 <NA>        <NA>          <NA>             #> # ℹ 40,990 more rows #> # ℹ 66 more variables: street_name <chr>, street_type <chr>, #> #   full_address <chr>, neighbourhood_area <chr>, market_region <chr>, #> #   total_living_area <int>, building_type <chr>, basement <chr>, #> #   basement_finish <chr>, year_built <int>, rooms <int>, #> #   air_conditioning <chr>, fire_place <chr>, attached_garage <chr>, #> #   detached_garage <chr>, pool <chr>, number_floors_condo <int>, …

title: wpgdata
text: easiest way directly R: Alternatively, browse City Winnipeg Open Data Portal copy ID OData V4 endpoint URL:
code: peg_catalogue() |>   filter(grepl("your search term", name, ignore.case = TRUE)) |>   select(name, id, category) https://data.winnipeg.ca/api/odata/v4/d4mq-wa44                                       ^^^^^^^^^^                                       dataset ID

title: wpgdata
text: MIT © Myo Minn Oo

what: Get all rows from a Winnipeg Open Data Portal dataset — peg_all
title: Get all rows from a Winnipeg Open Data Portal dataset — peg_all
text: Automatically paginates pages dataset using @odata.nextLink. large datasets, use max_pages cap number pages fetched.

title: Get all rows from a Winnipeg Open Data Portal dataset — peg_all
code: peg_all(dataset_id, max_pages = 10)

title: Get all rows from a Winnipeg Open Data Portal dataset — peg_all
text: dataset_id character string dataset ID e.g. "d4mq-wa44" max_pages integer specifying maximum number pages fetch. Defaults 10. Set Inf fetch pages limit.

title: Get all rows from a Winnipeg Open Data Portal dataset — peg_all
text: tibble rows retrieved

title: Get all rows from a Winnipeg Open Data Portal dataset — peg_all
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # fetch with default safety cap of 10 pages peg_all("d4mq-wa44")  # fetch all rows with no limit peg_all("d4mq-wa44", max_pages = Inf)  # fetch up to 3 pages peg_all("d4mq-wa44", max_pages = 3) } # }

what: List all datasets from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_catalogue
title: List all datasets from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_catalogue
text: Retrieves catalogue publicly available datasets City Winnipeg Open Data Portal (data.winnipeg.ca) using Socrata Discovery API.

title: List all datasets from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_catalogue
code: peg_catalogue(limit = 200)

title: List all datasets from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_catalogue
text: limit integer specifying maximum number datasets return. Defaults 200. Set NULL fetch available datasets.

title: List all datasets from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_catalogue
text: tibble one row per dataset containing: name Dataset name id Dataset ID — use peg_get(), peg_query(), peg_all(), peg_metadata(), peg_info() description Dataset description category Dataset category updated_at Date time last update row_count Number downloads url Direct URL dataset data.winnipeg.ca

title: List all datasets from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_catalogue
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # list all available datasets peg_catalogue()  # fetch everything peg_catalogue(limit = NULL)  # find datasets by category library(dplyr) peg_catalogue() |>   filter(category == "Transportation")  # search by name peg_catalogue() |>   filter(grepl("assessment", name, ignore.case = TRUE))  # count datasets by category peg_catalogue() |>   count(category, sort = TRUE) } # }

title: Get a dataset from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_get
text: Get dataset Winnipeg Open Data Portal

title: Get a dataset from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_get
code: peg_get(dataset_id)

title: Get a dataset from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_get
text: dataset_id character string dataset ID e.g. "d4mq-wa44"

title: Get a dataset from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_get
text: tibble dataset

title: Get a dataset from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_get
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ peg_get("d4mq-wa44") } # }

what: Get dataset-level information from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_info
title: Get dataset-level information from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_info
text: Get dataset-level information Winnipeg Open Data Portal

title: Get dataset-level information from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_info
code: peg_info(dataset_id)

title: Get dataset-level information from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_info
text: dataset_id character string dataset ID e.g. "d4mq-wa44"

title: Get dataset-level information from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_info
text: tibble one row containing dataset-level metadata including name, description, category, timestamps, counts, tags, license, provenance.

title: Get dataset-level information from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_info
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ peg_info("d4mq-wa44") } # }

what: Get column names and types for a Winnipeg Open Data dataset — peg_metadata
title: Get column names and types for a Winnipeg Open Data dataset — peg_metadata
text: Get column names types Winnipeg Open Data dataset

title: Get column names and types for a Winnipeg Open Data dataset — peg_metadata
code: peg_metadata(dataset_id)

title: Get column names and types for a Winnipeg Open Data dataset — peg_metadata
text: dataset_id character string dataset ID e.g. "d4mq-wa44"

title: Get column names and types for a Winnipeg Open Data dataset — peg_metadata
text: tibble columns name, field_name, type, description. Use field_name values peg_query() filtering selecting columns.

title: Get column names and types for a Winnipeg Open Data dataset — peg_metadata
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # look up field names before querying peg_metadata("d4mq-wa44")  # then use field_name in peg_query() peg_query("d4mq-wa44", filter = total_assessed_value > 500000) } # }

what: Query a dataset from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_query
title: Query a dataset from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_query
text: Query dataset Winnipeg Open Data Portal

title: Query a dataset from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_query
code: peg_query(   dataset_id,   filter = NULL,   select = NULL,   top = NULL,   skip = NULL,   orderby = NULL )

title: Query a dataset from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_query
text: dataset_id character string dataset ID e.g. "d4mq-wa44" filter filter expression, either raw OData string e.g. "total_assessed_value gt 500000" R expression e.g. total_assessed_value > 500000. Use peg_metadata() find valid field names. select character vector field names return e.g. c("roll_number", "total_assessed_value") top integer specifying maximum number rows return skip integer specifying number rows skip orderby character string specifying field sort e.g. "total_assessed_value desc" "total_assessed_value asc"

title: Query a dataset from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_query
text: tibble queried dataset

title: Query a dataset from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_query
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # use peg_metadata() first to find valid field names peg_metadata("d4mq-wa44")  # R expression filter peg_query("d4mq-wa44", filter = total_assessed_value > 500000)  # raw OData filter string peg_query("d4mq-wa44", filter = "total_assessed_value gt 500000")  # select specific columns peg_query("d4mq-wa44", select = c("roll_number", "total_assessed_value"))  # combine filter, select, orderby peg_query("d4mq-wa44",   filter  = total_assessed_value > 500000,   select  = c("roll_number", "total_assessed_value"),   orderby = "total_assessed_value desc",   top     = 100 )  # skip first 500 rows peg_query("d4mq-wa44", skip = 500, top = 100) } # }

title: wpgdata 0.2.0
text: peg_catalogue() — list datasets available City Winnipeg Open Data Portal name, ID, category, last updated date.

title: wpgdata 0.2.0
text: Added get_catalogue_count(), fetch_catalogue_page(), parse_catalogue() internal helpers utils.R. Imported rlang::.data pronoun suppress R CMD check notes dplyr::mutate() calls.

title: wpgdata 0.1.0
text: peg_get() — fetch first page dataset peg_query() — filter, select, sort paginate datasets peg_all() — download rows automatic pagination peg_metadata() — retrieve column names types peg_info() — retrieve dataset-level information Supports R expressions raw OData strings peg_query() Automatic pagination detection progress bar peg_all()
